RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 408-B, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
Representative John Simmons, a Republican, represents parts of Franklin County (District 109) in the Missouri House of Representatives. He was first elected to his first two-year term in November 2018, where he served two successive terms before returning in November of 2024 for a third term.
Simmons built a successful private practice in chiropractic medicine in Washington, earning his degree from Logan University of Chiropractic in 1991. His background in healthcare, running a small business, involvement in Rotary Club of Washington, and New Haven and Washington Chambers, provides him with valuable insight into issues affecting his community.
With prior familiarity with the legislative process, Simmons is no stranger to advocacy and policy. Simmons himself sponsored the Photo ID Election Integrity Bill in 2022 and co-sponsored the Heartbeat Bill in 2019.
Simmons has received numerous accolades, including Legislator of the Year award from the Missouri Chiropractic Physicians Association (MCPA) in recognition of his contributions to his profession and community; and Legislator Champion Award from The Foundation for Government Accountability for his work with election law.
Simmons is married to his wife, Nanci, and together they have three children: Reagan, Madison, and Noah. They attend St. Gertrude Church in Krakow. Family is central to his life, and he is deeply committed to serving his constituents and improving the quality of life for all Missourians.

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Designates March 16th of each year as "Election Judges and Poll Workers Appreciation Day" in Missouri
Modifies provisions relating to ballot summary language
Authorizes income tax deductions for employee compensation received as overtime pay or tips
Modifies Missouri driver's licenses and nondriver's licenses to include a symbol that indicates that the holder of the license is a United States citizen
Modifies provisions relating to civil penalties for violations of federally mandated natural gas safety standards
Changes the law regarding political party primary elections
Modifies provisions for foreign influence on ballot measures
Creates new provisions related to infrastructure security
Prohibits state or local licensing requirements for certain industrial mechanical maintenance work
Requires the release of personal property located within abandoned property that has been towed
Proposes a constitutional amendment modifying requirements for votes required to pass constitutional amendments
Proposes a constitutional amendment modifying requirements for votes required to pass constitutional amendments
Proposes a constitutional amendment that modifies requirements for votes required to pass constitutional amendments
Modifies requirements for votes required to pass constitutional amendments
Proposes a constitutional amendment that modifies term limits for members of the general assembly and the number of members of the house of representatives
Proposes a constitutional amendment that modifies term limits for members of the general assembly

CoSponsoredBill.LongTitle: Modifies provisions relating to income tax
Prohibits the use of fetal organs or tissue resulting from elective abortions for medical, scientific, research, experimental, or therapeutic purposes or any other use
Creates the "Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act"
Modifies provisions relating to income tax on tips
Allows the court to enter a judgment of dissolution of marriage or legal separation if a person is pregnant
Creates provisions relating to health care provider participation in health insurance plans
Prohibits temporary assistance for needy families (TANF) benefit cards from being used at ATMs or to access cash, and limits the items that may be purchased with TANF benefits
Requires a state agency to repeal two existing rules before enacting a new one
Changes the law regarding firearm concealed carry permits
Establishes the "Anti-Red Flag Gun Seizure Act"
Establishes the "Anti-Red Flag Gun Seizure Act"
Establishes written parental consent requirements for individualized education programs (IEPs)
Creates provisions relating to health care provider participation in health insurance plans
Modifies the election date, terms of office, and term limits for members of school boards
Establishes written parental consent requirements for individualized education programs (IEPs)
Prohibits the use of automated traffic enforcement systems and the use of automated license plate readers
Requires the general assembly to approve proposed administrative rules
Modifies provisions relating to firearms
Prohibits state departments from spending money on diversity, equity, and inclusion initiatives
Modifies the duration of unemployment benefits based on the unemployment rate
Designates the "Lloyd Memorial Highway" in Newton County
Establishes the "Fair Tax Act of 2025" which replaces the state individual and corporate income tax and the estate tax with a fair tax based on all new retail sales and services
Requires an audit of all state departments every four years
Designates April 27th each year as "Ulysses S. Grant Day" in Missouri
Reduces the assessment percentage of tangible personal property over a period of years
Prohibits educational institutions from using state funding for diversity, equity, and inclusion purposes
Creates a new joint committee on government efficiency
Changes the law regarding primary elections
Reestablishes the "Second Amendment Preservation Act"
Prohibits the use of fetal organs or tissue resulting from abortions for medical, scientific, research, experimental, or therapeutic purposes or any other use

Proposes a constitutional amendment to impose an appropriation spending limitation and to establish the "Tax Reform Fund" to be used to fund budgetary shortfalls, subject to an appropriation limitation, implement sales tax changes, and allow for certain taxation changes based on revenue triggers, by general law
Proposes a constitutional amendment that modifies provisions relating to constitutional amendments
Proposes a constitutional amendment relating to certain sexual offenses involving children
Proposes a constitutional amendment establishing provisions relating to reproductive health care
Proposes a constitutional amendment establishing provisions relating to reproductive health care
Proposes a constitutional amendment that establishes provisions relating to reproductive health care
Proposes a constitutional amendment that requires a majority vote in each of 3/4 of the counties in the state to pass any constitutional measure submitted to the people
Urges the states to compel Congress to propose an amendment to repeal the Sixteenth Amendment of the United States Constitution and return taxation authority to the states
